messages.author: George-GM1DSV
messages.text: I am slightly concerned that MQTT is being used by 3rd parties to monitor Meshcore over the Internet under the guise of performance monitoring. Surely this defeats the ethos of an off-grid communication system and why I walked away from Meshtastic. Nobody knows if their repeater is being covertly monitored by another without advert, and their mesh traffic being harvested for less than desirable uses! Just let that idea sink in please, the concept of off-grid is destroyed as soon as the internet in involved directly in the system. WHAT GIVES??
messages.content_html: I am slightly concerned that MQTT is being used by 3rd parties to monitor Meshcore over the Internet under the guise of performance monitoring. Surely this defeats the ethos of an off-grid communication system and why I walked away from Meshtastic. Nobody knows if their repeater is being covertly monitored by another without advert, and their mesh traffic being harvested for less than desirable uses! Just let that idea sink in please, the concept of off-grid is destroyed as soon as the internet in involved directly in the system. WHAT GIVES??
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: George-GM1DSV
messages.text: Not of it is kept off-line and operated the caution. Online tracking of Meshcore makes it no better than WhatsApp or Facebook. I'm certainly doing what I can to keep my mesh off-grid and that's how it is staying. I left Meshtastic for that very reason. Off-grid is simply that. I have no issue with the Internet being used to build the tools to allow online disconnection, that is good sense to ise a facility designed to control us to make a system of relative freedom.

messages.author: ka.RFz
messages.text: Exactly. If you want private comms and your location is kept private then don't use public channels and don't advertise your location, plain and simple. As someone who maintains dozens of repeaters I appreciate 1-way (northbound) MQTT raw packets going to services like analyzer.letsmesh.sh as a means of statistically reporting, performance monitoring and a way for us to improve the network by packet analysis. For example, in my area our mesh is huge. Just the other day we started seeing a misconfigured repeater that was corrupting packets. Without the analyzer site it would be difficult otherwise to pinpoint which possible repeater was involved and how its traffic was propagating onto the mesh. These MQTT observers are just that, observing. They have no bearing on how the mesh operates in an off grid scenario from a functional standpoint if the internet were to disappear.
messages.content_html: Exactly. If you want private comms and your location is kept private then don't use public channels and don't advertise your location, plain and simple. These MQTT observers are just that, observing. They have no bearing on how the mesh operates in an off grid scenario from a functional standpoint if the internet were to disappear.
